examples.title: ExpandoObject as a flexible property bag
examples.description: `ExpandoObject` lets you attach properties at runtime and read them back through a `dynamic` variable — useful when shape is unknown at compile time.

examples.title: dynamic vs. reflection
examples.description: Calling a method through reflection requires several lines of plumbing. `dynamic` collapses that to a single, readable call at the cost of compile-time verification.

relatedFeatures.reason: Named and optional parameters were introduced in the same C# 4.0 release and frequently appear alongside dynamic in COM interop scenarios.
Pattern matching offers a type-safe alternative for branching on unknown shapes — reach for it before resorting to dynamic.
Nullable reference types show how the compiler can track more safety information; dynamic goes the opposite direction, so understanding both clarifies the trade-off.
